sections.paragraph:
εὐσυνείδητος, ον (s. συνείδησις) with a good conscience (M. Ant. 6, 30, 15) εὐσυνείδητον εἶναι have a good conscience IMg 4. ἔν τινι about someone IPhld 6:3. Cp. εὐσυνείδητον πρᾶγμα ποιῶν Sb I, 4426, 12 (III a.d.).—DELG s.v. οἶδα.

  1. Ant. M. Ant. = Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, II a.d.—List 5

IMg IMg = Ignatius to the Magnesians—List 1

IPhld IPhld = Ignatius to the Philadelphians—List 1

Sb Sb = Sammelbuch griechischer Urkunden aus Aegypten—Lists 3, 4

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 413
